title: senecio andinus
intro: Senecio andinus, also known as Bolivian groundsel, is a flowering plant species native to the Andes mountains in South America. The plant contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including as a treatment for fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Senecio andinus include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infection. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
